words.definition_en: next to or near something else
words.example: We work in adjacent rooms.
words.example_vi: Chúng tôi làm việc trong các phòng kế bên nhau.

words.definition_en: to fasten, join, or connect something
words.example: You need to attach the handle to the drawer using these screws.
words.example_vi: Bạn cần gắn tay cầm vào ngăn kéo bằng những con vít này.

words.definition_en: to develop a close connection or strong relationship with someone; a strong feeling of friendship, love, or shared beliefs
words.example: The experience helped the team members to bond.
words.example_vi: Trải nghiệm đó đã giúp các thành viên trong nhóm gắn kết với nhau hơn.

words.definition_en: to live or exist together at the same time or in the same place
words.example: Different cultures can coexist peacefully within a single society.
words.example_vi: Các nền văn hóa khác nhau có thể cùng chung sống hòa bình trong một xã hội duy nhất.

words.definition_en: logical and consistent, easy to understand
words.example: The government lacks a coherent strategy for energy.
words.example_vi: Chính phủ thiếu một chiến lược năng lượng mạch lạc.

words.definition_en: able to exist, live, or work together without problems
words.example: They are not very compatible, they argue all the time.
words.example_vi: Họ không hợp nhau lắm, họ cãi nhau suốt ngày.

words.definition_en: to consist of or to be made up of
words.example: The course comprises ten individual modules.
words.example_vi: Khóa học bao gồm mười học phần riêng biệt.

words.definition_en: an agreement in an argument in which the people involved reduce their demands or change their opinion in order to agree
words.example: It is hoped that a compromise will be reached in the next few days.
words.example_vi: Hy vọng rằng một sự thỏa hiệp sẽ đạt được trong vài ngày tới.

words.definition_en: a state of disagreement or argument between people, groups, countries etc.
words.example: The Government is in conflict with the unions over pay.
words.example_vi: Chính phủ đang xung đột với các công đoàn về vấn đề lương bổng.

words.definition_en: to face, meet, or deal with a difficult situation or person
words.example: I'm going to confront Roy about his behaviour.
words.example_vi: Tôi sẽ đối chất với Roy về hành vi của anh ta.

words.definition_en: always behaving or happening in a similar, especially positive, way
words.example: Your behavior is not consistent with what you said last week.
words.example_vi: Hành vi của bạn không nhất quán với những gì bạn đã nói tuần trước.

words.definition_en: to say that what someone else has said is wrong and that the opposite is true
words.example: The witness's story contradicted the police report.
words.example_vi: Câu chuyện của nhân chứng mâu thuẫn với báo cáo của cảnh sát.

words.definition_en: very different from each other
words.example: They have two very contrasting styles of management.
words.example_vi: Họ có hai phong cách quản lý rất tương phản.

words.definition_en: to work together with someone else in order to achieve something
words.example: The two companies are cooperating on a joint venture.
words.example_vi: Hai công ty đang hợp tác trong một liên doanh.

words.definition_en: to match or be similar or equal to something else
words.example: Your answer should correspond to the one in the back of the book.
words.example_vi: Câu trả lời của bạn phải khớp với câu trả lời ở cuối sách.

words.definition_en: an argument or disagreement, especially one that lasts a long time
words.example: The union is in dispute with management over pay.
words.example_vi: Công đoàn đang tranh chấp với ban quản lý về vấn đề lương bổng.

words.definition_en: to notice or understand the difference between two things
words.example: It's difficult to distinguish between the two brothers.
words.example_vi: Thật khó để phân biệt giữa hai anh em.

words.definition_en: including many different types of people or things
words.example: The subjects they study are very diverse.
words.example_vi: Các môn học họ nghiên cứu rất đa dạng.

words.definition_en: the legal ending of a marriage
words.example: They got a divorce after five years of marriage.
words.example_vi: Họ đã ly hôn sau năm năm chung sống.

words.definition_en: having the same amount, value, purpose, qualities, etc.
words.example: There is no equivalent for that word in English.
words.example_vi: Không có từ tương đương cho từ đó trong tiếng Anh.

words.definition_en: to prevent someone or something from entering a place or taking part in an activity
words.example: The report does not exclude the possibility of human error.
words.example_vi: Báo cáo không loại trừ khả năng do sai sót của con người.

words.definition_en: coming from or relating to the outside
words.example: The skin protects the body from external infection.
words.example_vi: Da bảo vệ cơ thể khỏi bị nhiễm trùng từ bên ngoài.

words.definition_en: to recognize someone or something and say who or what they are; to feel that you can understand and share the feelings of somebody else
words.example: She identified the man as her attacker. / I identify with the characters in the film.
words.example_vi: Cô ấy đã nhận dạng người đàn ông đó là kẻ tấn công mình. / Tôi thấy mình trong các nhân vật của bộ phim.

words.definition_en: necessary and important as a part of a whole
words.example: Vegetables are an integral part of our diet.
words.example_vi: Rau củ là một phần thiết yếu trong chế độ ăn uống của chúng ta.

words.definition_en: to combine two or more things so that they work together; to mix with and join a society or a group of people
words.example: It's difficult for immigrants to integrate into the local community.
words.example_vi: Thật khó để những người nhập cư hòa nhập vào cộng đồng địa phương.

words.definition_en: to involve yourself in a situation when your involvement is not wanted or is not helpful
words.example: I wish my mother-in-law would stop interfering in our lives.
words.example_vi: Tôi ước mẹ chồng mình đừng can thiệp vào cuộc sống của chúng tôi nữa.

words.definition_en: being between two other related things, levels, or points
words.example: This course is for students of intermediate level.
words.example_vi: Khóa học này dành cho sinh viên trình độ trung cấp.

words.definition_en: inside the body or within an organization
words.example: The bank is conducting an internal investigation.
words.example_vi: Ngân hàng đang tiến hành một cuộc điều tra nội bộ.

words.definition_en: to intentionally become involved in a difficult situation in order to improve it
words.example: The government had to intervene to prevent a civil war.
words.example_vi: Chính phủ đã phải can thiệp để ngăn chặn một cuộc nội chiến.

words.definition_en: having a very close friendship or personal relationship
words.example: They are intimate friends.
words.example_vi: Họ là những người bạn thân thiết.

words.definition_en: to include someone or something in an activity
words.example: The job involves a lot of travelling.
words.example_vi: Công việc này đòi hỏi phải đi lại nhiều.

words.definition_en: belonging to or shared by two or more people
words.example: The project was a joint effort between the two schools.
words.example_vi: Dự án là một nỗ lực chung giữa hai trường.

words.definition_en: to say that someone or something is similar to someone or something else
words.example: She likened the experience to a nightmare.
words.example_vi: Cô ấy ví trải nghiệm đó như một cơn ác mộng.

words.definition_en: a connection between two people, things, or ideas; to make a connection
words.example: Police suspect there is a link between the two crimes.
words.example_vi: Cảnh sát nghi ngờ có mối liên hệ giữa hai tội ác này.

words.definition_en: to combine or join together
words.example: The two companies merged to form a new giant.
words.example_vi: Hai công ty đã sáp nhập để tạo thành một gã khổng lồ mới.

words.definition_en: (of two or more people) feeling the same emotion, or doing the same thing to or for each other
words.example: The agreement was based on mutual respect.
words.example_vi: Thỏa thuận dựa trên sự tôn trọng lẫn nhau.

words.definition_en: to try to reach an agreement or compromise by discussion
words.example: We are negotiating with the company for a higher salary.
words.example_vi: Chúng tôi đang thương lượng với công ty để có mức lương cao hơn.

words.definition_en: belonging to the same family; connected
words.definition_vi: có liên quan, có họ hàng
words.example: Are you related to the famous actor?
words.example_vi: Bạn có họ hàng với nam diễn viên nổi tiếng đó không?

words.definition_en: a member of your family; considered in relation or in proportion to something else
words.example: She has several relatives living in the city.
words.example_vi: Tôi nhận được một email từ một người họ hàng xa.

words.definition_en: the fact that two people or things look like each other
words.example: There's no resemblance between Gary and Mark.
words.example_vi: Không có điểm nào giống nhau giữa Gary và Mark cả.

words.definition_en: a person that you have met but do not know well
words.example: I wouldn't call him a friend, more of an acquaintance.
words.example_vi: Tôi không gọi anh ta là bạn, đúng hơn là một người quen.

words.definition_en: to legally take another person's child into your own family
words.example: They were unable to have children of their own, so they decided to adopt.
words.example_vi: Họ không thể có con nên đã quyết định nhận con nuôi.

words.definition_en: a person related to you who lived a long time ago
words.example: My ancestors came from Italy in the 19th century.
words.example_vi: Tổ tiên tôi đến từ Ý vào thế kỷ 19.

words.definition_en: a person who is a member of a particular country
words.example: He wanted to become an American citizen as soon as he could.
words.example_vi: Anh ấy muốn trở thành công dân Mỹ càng sớm càng tốt.

words.definition_en: a person you spend a lot of time with or travel with
words.example: She was my constant companion during those difficult times.
words.example_vi: Cô ấy là người bạn đồng hành luôn bên cạnh tôi trong những thời điểm khó khăn đó.

words.definition_en: a person who relies on another, especially a family member, for financial support
words.example: He's got a large number of dependants, including three children.
words.example_vi: Anh ấy có nhiều người phụ thuộc, bao gồm cả ba đứa con.

words.definition_en: a person who is related to you and who lives after you, such as your child or grandchild
words.example: I'm a descendant of Isaac Newton.
words.example_vi: Tôi là hậu duệ của Isaac Newton.

words.definition_en: to be able to understand how someone else feels
words.example: It's difficult to empathise with her when you haven't experienced anything similar.
words.example_vi: Thật khó để đồng cảm với cô ấy khi bạn chưa từng trải qua điều gì tương tự.

words.definition_en: an energetic happy person who enjoys being with other people
words.example: Guy's a real extrovert; he'll chat to anybody.
words.example_vi: Guy là một người hướng ngoại thực thụ; anh ấy sẽ trò chuyện với bất cứ ai.

words.definition_en: to take care of a child, usually for a limited time, without being the child's legal parent
words.example: My parents often fostered children for a few months.
words.example_vi: Cha mẹ tôi thường nhận nuôi dưỡng trẻ em trong vài tháng.

words.definition_en: a person who has the legal right and responsibility of taking care of someone who cannot take care of themselves
words.example: You have to get your legal guardian to sign the form.
words.example_vi: Bạn phải nhờ người giám hộ hợp pháp ký vào mẫu đơn.

words.definition_en: someone who is shy, quiet, and prefers to spend time alone
words.example: I suppose I'm something of an introvert; I'm quite shy.
words.example_vi: Tôi cho rằng mình hơi hướng nội; tôi khá nhút nhát.

words.definition_en: a person you are closely involved with in a business or relationship
words.example: The invitation didn't say that partners were invited.
words.example_vi: Lời mời không nói rằng bạn đời cũng được mời.

words.definition_en: a person who is the same age or has the same social position
words.definition_vi: người cùng trang lứa, đồng nghiệp
words.example: It's important not to be different from your peers.
words.example_vi: Thanh thiếu niên thường cảm thấy bị áp lực bởi bạn bè cùng trang lứa.

words.definition_en: someone who had a job or a position before someone else
words.example: My predecessor in the job left things in a real mess.
words.example_vi: Người tiền nhiệm của tôi trong công việc đã để lại mọi thứ trong một mớ hỗn độn.

words.definition_en: a brother or sister
words.example: I never really got on with any of my siblings.
words.example_vi: Tôi chưa bao giờ thực sự hòa thuận với bất kỳ anh chị em nào của mình.

words.definition_en: a person's husband or wife
words.example: Write the name of your spouse in the space provided.
words.example_vi: Viết tên vợ hoặc chồng của bạn vào chỗ trống được cung cấp.

words.definition_en: the woman who has married your father but is not your mother
words.example: I don't get on that well with my stepmother.
words.example_vi: Tôi không hòa hợp lắm với mẹ kế của mình.

words.definition_en: someone or something that takes the place of another person or thing
words.example: I'm sure my successor will carry on the good work.
words.example_vi: Tôi chắc chắn rằng người kế nhiệm tôi sẽ tiếp tục công việc tốt đẹp này.

words.definition_en: to understand and care about someone else's suffering
words.example: I do sympathise with Kim, but I can't say I understand what she's going through.
words.example_vi: Tôi rất thông cảm với Kim, nhưng tôi không thể nói rằng tôi hiểu những gì cô ấy đang trải qua.

words.definition_en: reply rudely to someone who has more authority than you
words.example: Don't you dare answer me back!
words.example_vi: Con không được phép cãi lại ta!

words.definition_en: invite someone to go with you to a cinema, restaurant, etc because you want to start a romantic or sexual relationship with them
words.example: I'd like to ask Melanie out on a date.
words.example_vi: Tôi muốn mời Melanie đi chơi.

words.definition_en: end a relationship; break something into smaller pieces; end an event
words.definition_vi: chia tay; đập nhỏ; tan họp
words.example: I haven't seen much of Cameron since he and Nicola broke up.
words.example_vi: Tôi không gặp Cameron nhiều kể từ khi anh ấy và Nicola chia tay.

words.definition_en: make someone or something show a quality that they have; produce a new product and start to sell it
words.definition_vi: làm nổi bật; tung ra sản phẩm
words.example: The crisis brought out the best in him. / They're bringing out a new album.
words.example_vi: Cuộc khủng hoảng đã bộc lộ những phẩm chất tốt nhất trong anh ấy. / Họ sắp tung ra một album mới.

words.definition_en: create a situation in which people meet and do something together
words.example: The disaster brought the community together.
words.example_vi: Thảm họa đã gắn kết cộng đồng lại với nhau.

words.definition_en: meet someone unexpectedly; accidentally hit against something
words.example: Guess who I bumped into in town today?
words.example_vi: Đoán xem hôm nay tôi đã tình cờ gặp ai ở trong thị trấn nào?

words.definition_en: stop something from having any effect
words.example: The gains and losses should cancel each other out.
words.example_vi: Các khoản lãi và lỗ sẽ bù trừ cho nhau.

words.definition_en: cause a disagreement or argument between people
words.example: She is determined not to let anything come between them.
words.example_vi: Cô ấy quyết tâm không để bất cứ điều gì xen vào giữa họ.

words.definition_en: move to a particular place at the same time as a lot of other people
words.example: The children crowded around the teacher to see the book.
words.example_vi: Những đứa trẻ vây quanh giáo viên để xem cuốn sách.

words.definition_en: frequently exist together; seem good or attractive in combination
words.definition_vi: đi đôi với nhau; hợp nhau
words.example: Those two things often go together. / Do you think this tie and shirt go together?
words.example_vi: Hai thứ đó thường đi đôi với nhau. / Bạn có nghĩ chiếc cà vạt này và chiếc áo sơ mi này hợp nhau không?

words.definition_en: come together with someone, either unexpectedly or as planned
words.example: Why don't we meet up here again on the 12th?
words.example_vi: Tại sao chúng ta không gặp lại nhau ở đây vào ngày 12 nhỉ?

words.definition_en: talk more about your personal feelings; open a locked door/building; make it easier to travel/do business
words.definition_vi: mở lòng, tâm sự; mở khóa; mở rộng (cơ hội)
words.example: He started to open up about his feelings.
words.example_vi: Anh ấy bắt đầu cởi mở hơn về cảm xúc của mình.

words.definition_en: keep treating someone badly or unfairly, especially by criticising them
words.definition_vi: bắt nạt, chỉ trích bất công
words.example: Why do you always pick on me?
words.example_vi: Tại sao bạn luôn bắt nạt tôi vậy?

words.definition_en: try to find out someone's opinions, ideas, feelings, etc by talking to them
words.example: You should sound your parents out and see what they think.
words.example_vi: Bạn nên hỏi dò ý kiến bố mẹ xem họ nghĩ gì.

words.definition_en: look or behave like an older relative
words.example: Michael really takes after his grandfather.
words.example_vi: Michael thực sự rất giống ông nội mình.

words.definition_en: begin to like someone or something; start doing something as a habit
words.definition_vi: bắt đầu thích; bắt đầu một thói quen
words.example: I didn't take to my stepbrother at first. / He's taken to staying up late.
words.example_vi: Lúc đầu tôi không có cảm tình với anh kế của mình. / Anh ấy bắt đầu có thói quen thức khuya.

words.definition_en: born to do, born on/in, born of, born into, born and bred, born-again, newborn
words.definition_vi: sinh ra (các cụm từ liên quan)
words.example: He was born into a wealthy family.
words.example_vi: Anh ấy sinh ra trong một gia đình giàu có.

words.definition_en: as a child, only child, a child of, child abuse, childcare, child's play, child support
words.definition_vi: đứa trẻ (các cụm từ liên quan)
words.example: This maths problem is child's play.
words.example_vi: Bài toán này dễ như ăn cháo vậy.

words.definition_en: have sth in common (with sb), common for sb/sth to do, common to, common language, the common people, common practice
words.definition_vi: chung, phổ biến (các cụm từ liên quan)
words.example: We have a lot in common.
words.example_vi: Chúng tôi có rất nhiều điểm chung.

words.definition_en: of equal size/quality/value, equal in size/quality/value, equal to, roughly equal (to), equal rights
words.definition_vi: bằng nhau, công bằng (các cụm từ liên quan)
words.example: They recognize that they are of equal value.
words.example_vi: Họ công nhận rằng họ có giá trị ngang nhau.

words.definition_en: have/start a family, nuclear family, extended family, single-parent family, a family of, in the family, one of the family, family tree, family name, family values
words.definition_vi: gia đình (các cụm từ liên quan)
words.example: She very quickly became one of the family.
words.example_vi: Cô ấy nhanh chóng trở thành một thành viên trong gia đình.

words.definition_en: feature sb/sth in, a feature of, distinguishing feature, safety features, feature film, feature writer
words.definition_vi: đặc điểm (các cụm từ liên quan)
words.example: The car has some excellent safety features.
words.example_vi: Chiếc xe có một số tính năng an toàn tuyệt vời.

words.definition_en: make friends (with), close/good/great friend, old friend, friend of the family, family friend, circle of friends, friends with
words.definition_vi: bạn bè (các cụm từ liên quan)
words.example: I'm still friends with my ex-boyfriend.
words.example_vi: Tôi vẫn là bạn với bạn trai cũ của mình.

words.definition_en: the older/younger generation, generation gap, Generation X, future generations
words.definition_vi: thế hệ (các cụm từ liên quan)
words.example: The generation gap seems to be widening.
words.example_vi: Khoảng cách thế hệ dường như đang ngày càng lớn dần.

words.definition_en: human behaviour, human being, human error, human nature, human race, human rights
words.definition_vi: con người (các cụm từ liên quan)
words.example: It's only human nature to want to settle down.
words.example_vi: Bản tính tự nhiên của con người là muốn ổn định cuộc sống.

words.definition_en: love to do, love doing, give/send your love to, (fall) in love (with sb), love for, true love, love at first sight, love affair, loved ones
words.definition_vi: tình yêu (các cụm từ liên quan)
words.example: They fell in love at first sight.
words.example_vi: Họ đã yêu nhau ngay từ cái nhìn đầu tiên.

words.definition_en: (related) by marriage, marriage guidance, marriage vows, marriage of convenience
words.definition_vi: hôn nhân (các cụm từ liên quan)
words.example: She's my aunt by marriage.
words.example_vi: Cô ấy là dì/mợ (vợ của cậu/chú/bác) của tôi.

words.definition_en: the mother of, mother country, mother-in-law, motherland, Mother Nature, Mother's Day, mother-to-be, mother tongue
words.example: Italian is her mother tongue.
words.example_vi: Tiếng Ý là tiếng mẹ đẻ của cô ấy.

words.definition_en: in the national interest, national anthem, national costume/dress, national debt, national holiday
words.definition_vi: quốc gia (các cụm từ liên quan)
words.example: Tomorrow is a national holiday.
words.example_vi: Ngày mai là một ngày lễ quốc gia.

words.definition_en: go native, native to, a native of, native speaker, non-native speaker, native land, native species
words.definition_vi: bản ngữ, bản địa (các cụm từ liên quan)
words.example: Native speakers of English often speak too fast.
words.example_vi: Người bản ngữ tiếng Anh thường nói quá nhanh.

words.definition_en: near to (doing sth), the near future, from near and far, a near thing, the nearest thing to, your nearest and dearest
words.example: People came from near and far to attend the wedding.
words.example_vi: Mọi người từ khắp nơi đổ về để tham dự đám cưới.

words.definition_en: do sth in person, meet sb in person
words.example: I've never met him in person.
words.example_vi: Tôi chưa bao giờ gặp trực tiếp anh ta.

words.definition_en: it's (all) relative, relative to, close/near relative, distant relative, a relative of yours, relative clause/pronoun
words.definition_vi: họ hàng, tương đối (các cụm từ liên quan)
words.example: I got an e-mail from a distant relative.
words.example_vi: Tôi nhận được một email từ một người họ hàng xa.

words.definition_en: respect sb for, respect sb as, gain/lose sb's respect, command/deserve/earn/win respect, treat sb with respect, with respect to, in this/that respect
words.definition_vi: tôn trọng (các cụm từ liên quan)
words.example: He treats his wife with respect.
words.example_vi: Anh ấy đối xử với vợ mình bằng sự tôn trọng.

words.definition_en: support doing sth, support sb (financially), support an idea, support a team, offer support, in support of
words.definition_vi: ủng hộ, hỗ trợ (các cụm từ liên quan)
words.example: We supported our son while he was at university.
words.example_vi: Chúng tôi đã nuôi nấng con trai mình khi nó còn học đại học.

words.definition_en: wedding anniversary, wedding cake, wedding ceremony, wedding dress, wedding invitation, wedding ring, wedding present
words.definition_vi: đám cưới (các cụm từ liên quan)
words.example: It's their wedding anniversary next week.
words.example_vi: Tuần tới là kỷ niệm ngày cưới của họ.

words.word: be born with a silver spoon in your mouth
words.definition_en: have advantages because you come from a rich family
words.definition_vi: sinh ra đã ở vạch đích / ngậm thìa vàng
words.example: She's never had to work; she was born with a silver spoon in her mouth.
words.example_vi: Cô ấy chưa bao giờ phải làm việc; cô ấy sinh ra đã ở vạch đích rồi.

words.definition_en: understand the way another person thinks because you often have the same ideas and opinions
words.definition_vi: cùng tần số / có cùng suy nghĩ
words.example: Vivian and I usually agree; we're on the same wavelength.
words.example_vi: Vivian và tôi thường đồng ý với nhau; chúng tôi có cùng tần số.

words.word: get on like a house on fire
words.definition_en: become good friends very quickly and have a lot to talk about
words.definition_vi: nhanh chóng trở nên thân thiết
words.example: My brother and Jack got on like a house on fire when they first met.
words.example_vi: Anh trai tôi và Jack đã rất hợp nhau ngay lần đầu gặp mặt.

words.definition_en: used for saying that someone is annoyed/pleased with you
words.definition_vi: làm ai đó bực mình / hài lòng
words.example: I'm in his bad books because I forgot his birthday.
words.example_vi: Tôi bị anh ấy ghét vì tôi đã quên sinh nhật của anh ấy.

words.word: like two peas in a pod
words.definition_en: used for saying that two people look, behave or think exactly the same
words.example: Jill and Emily are like two peas in a pod.
words.example_vi: Jill và Emily giống nhau như đúc.

words.definition_en: have a good relationship with someone
words.definition_vi: có mối quan hệ tốt (với ai)
words.example: I'm on good terms with her sister.
words.example_vi: Tôi có mối quan hệ tốt với chị gái cô ấy.

words.definition_en: provide a sensible way of judging how important something is in comparison with other things
words.definition_vi: nhìn nhận khách quan / đặt điều gì đúng vào bối cảnh
words.example: That tragic accident really puts your own problems in perspective.
words.example_vi: Tai nạn thảm khốc đó thực sự khiến bạn phải nhìn nhận lại những vấn đề của chính mình.

words.definition_en: used for saying someone has been weak in the ways most people are weak and should not be blamed too much
words.definition_vi: con người ai cũng có lúc sai lầm
words.example: I know I made a mistake, but I'm only human.
words.example_vi: Tôi biết mình đã phạm sai lầm, nhưng tôi cũng chỉ là con người thôi.

words.definition_en: agree with someone or have the same opinion as them
words.definition_vi: có cùng quan điểm / đồng tình
words.example: I don't think we're ever going to see eye to eye on this issue.
words.example_vi: Tôi không nghĩ rằng chúng ta sẽ bao giờ có cùng quan điểm về vấn đề này.

words.definition_en: your relative
words.example: It's a shame to fall out with your own flesh and blood.
words.example_vi: Thật đáng xấu hổ khi lại bất hòa với chính người thân máu mủ của mình.

words.definition_en: disappear, reappear, (dis/re)appearance, apparition, apparent(ly)
appreciation, appreciable, appreciably, (un)appreciative(ly)
words.example_vi: Chúng tôi thực sự trân trọng tất cả sự giúp đỡ mà bạn dành cho chúng tôi.

words.definition_en: dissociate, association, associate, associated
words.definition_vi: liên tưởng, kết hợp (các từ phái sinh)
words.example_vi: Tôi nghĩ mình sẽ luôn gắn liền bài hát này với kỳ nghỉ của chúng ta ở Majorca.

words.definition_en: reattach, attachment, (un)attached
words.example_vi: Bạn cần gắn tay cầm vào ngăn kéo bằng những con vít này.

words.definition_en: characterise, characterisation, (un)characteristic, characterless
words.definition_vi: nhân vật, tính cách (các từ phái sinh)

words.definition_en: disconnect, reconnect, interconnect, (dis/re)connection, (inter)connecting, (un/dis/inter)connected, connector
familiarise, (un)familiarity, unfamiliar, familiarly
humanise, humanity, humanism, humanist, humanities, humanitarian, humane, humanly
words.example_vi: Bản tính tự nhiên của con người là muốn ổn định cuộc sống.

words.definition_en: individualise, individualisation, individuality, individualist, individually
inheritance, heritage, heredity, hereditary
intimacy, intimately
words.example_vi: Họ là những người bạn thân thiết.

words.definition_en: (dis)loyalty, disloyal, (dis)loyally
observation, observance, observer, observatory, observable, observant, observably
parenting, parentage, parenthood, parental(ly)
perception, perceptiveness, (im)perceptible, (im)perceptibly, perceptive(ly)
personalise, impersonate, personality, personnel, interpersonal, personalised, (im)personal(ly)
words.example_vi: Tôi chưa bao giờ gặp trực tiếp anh ta.

words.definition_en: racism, racist, interracial, racial(ly)
relation, relationship, (un)related, relative(ly)
(un)selfishness, selflessness, (un)selfish(ly), selfless(ly)
separation, (in)separable, separated, separately
sympathise, sympathiser, (un)sympathetic, (un)sympathetically
youngster, youth, youthful

exercises.label: A
exercises.instruction: Complete using the correct form of the words in the box.

items.sentence: It's not easy to ___ between Nick and Rick because they're identical twins.
items.explanation: 'Distinguish' means to tell the difference between two things/people.

items.sentence: Why do you always ___ me and say that what I'm saying isn't true?
items.explanation: 'Contradict' means to say the opposite of what someone else has said.

items.sentence: I love mountaineering, and I suppose I would ___ it to the excitement of winning the lottery.
items.explanation: 'Liken' means to compare one thing to another.

items.sentence: One of the reasons we grow to love characters in films is that we ___ with them.
items.explanation: 'Identify with' means to feel that you can understand and share someone's feelings.

items.sentence: You need to ___ the handle to the drawer using these screws.
items.explanation: 'Attach' means to fasten or join something.

items.sentence: I wouldn't ___ the possibility that you could be completely wrong on this.
items.explanation: 'Exclude' means to leave out or not consider a possibility.

items.sentence: Your answer to the maths problem should be the same as mine, but they don't ___ .
items.explanation: 'Correspond' means to match or be similar.

items.sentence: I'm going to ___ Roy about his behaviour because I think it's unacceptable.
items.explanation: 'Confront' means to face or deal with someone about a problem.

items.sentence: Don't you think that people who come to live in this country should ___ into our culture?
items.explanation: 'Integrate' means to mix with and join a society.

items.sentence: In some countries, children under 16 ___ the majority of the population.
items.explanation: 'Comprise' means to make up or consist of.

items.sentence: They say the price for the house is two hundred thousand, but I'm sure they'll ___ .
items.explanation: 'Negotiate' means to discuss something to reach an agreement.

items.sentence: I'm not ___ the facts - I just don't agree with your interpretation of them.
items.explanation: 'Dispute' means to argue against or disagree with something.

items.sentence: Our office is going to ___ with the one in the next town so some people will lose their jobs.
items.explanation: 'Merge' means to combine or join together.

items.sentence: Having a successful marriage ___ learning how to keep quiet at the right time.
items.explanation: 'Involve' means to include as a necessary part.

items.sentence: I couldn't believe it when Marcy accused me of ___ in her relationship with Joe.
items.explanation: 'Interfering' has a negative connotation of meddling where not wanted.

items.sentence: Do you think there might be a ___ between Angie's behaviour and the accident she had?
items.explanation: 'Link' is the standard word for a connection between two facts or situations.

items.sentence: Suddenly, I overheard Melissa and Alex talking about me in the ___ room.
items.explanation: 'Adjacent' means the room right next to the current one.

items.sentence: It's difficult to translate because there's no ___ for that word in English.
items.explanation: 'Equivalent' refers to something that has the same value or meaning.

items.sentence: Andrea and Theresa are so different! You couldn't have two more ___ sisters.
items.explanation: 'Contrasting' describes things that are very different from each other.

items.sentence: Keeping a relationship going is hard enough, without any ___ pressure from parents.
items.explanation: 'External' refers to pressure coming from the outside (not from within the couple).

items.sentence: Having your heart broken is an ___ part of growing up.
items.explanation: 'Integral' means a necessary and important part of a whole.

items.sentence: Getting divorced was a ___ decision, so I don't blame my ex-wife.
items.explanation: 'Joint' means shared or made by two or more people together.

items.sentence: But what you're saying now isn't ___ with what you said last week.
items.explanation: 'Consistent' means staying the same or matching previous statements.

items.sentence: You have to go through a number of ___ stages before you become an expert.
items.explanation: 'Intermediate' refers to the levels between beginning and advanced.

items.sentence: There's no ___ between Gary and Mark, even though they are brothers.
items.explanation: 'Resemblance' means looking similar to someone else.

items.sentence: There's no reason why people of different races can't ___ quite peacefully together.
items.explanation: 'Coexist' means to live together in the same place at the same time.

exercises.label: C
exercises.instruction: Complete using the correct form of the words in the box.

items.sentence: When people are in ___ , it can often help to talk to someone outside the relationship.
items.explanation: Refers to a state of disagreement.

items.sentence: When the ___ that holds people together weakens and arguments break out...
items.explanation: The connection or tie between people.

items.sentence: ...the ___ calm of a counsellor's office can make a big difference.
items.explanation: Meaning 'comparative' in this context.

items.sentence: It started life as the Marriage Guidance Council in 1943 and was founded by Dr Herbert Gray, who was concerned at the increase in ___ .
items.explanation: The legal ending of marriages.

items.sentence: They have dealt with a ___ range of problems over the years...
items.explanation: Meaning 'varied' or 'wide'.

items.sentence: ...from couples who feel that their personalities aren't ___ ...
items.explanation: Meaning they cannot exist together without problems.

items.sentence: ...to couples who have problems being ___ with each other.
items.explanation: Meaning close and personal.

items.sentence: Getting people to ___ with each other on rebuilding the relationship...
items.explanation: Meaning to work together.

items.sentence: ...and encouraging ___ respect often helps.
items.explanation: Respect felt by both sides for each other.

items.sentence: Often, people need to learn that they can't have exactly what they want and that ___ is necessary.
items.explanation: Meaning giving up some demands to reach agreement.

exercises.label: D
exercises.instruction: Complete using the words in the box.

items.sentence: I don't know Tony that well. I wouldn't call him a friend, more of an ___ , really.
items.explanation: Someone you know but not well.

items.sentence: I don't get on that well with my ___ . Since my parents got divorced and Dad married her...
items.explanation: The woman who married your father.

items.sentence: And remember that you have to get your legal ___ to sign it, giving you permission to go.
items.explanation: The person legally responsible for a child.

items.sentence: I suppose I'm something of an ___ . I'm quite shy, really, and don't like meeting new people.
items.explanation: A quiet person who prefers being alone.

items.sentence: If you're married, write the name of your ___ in the space provided.
items.explanation: A husband or wife.

items.sentence: Then I noticed her travelling ___ , a small woman of a similar age with four suitcases.
items.explanation: Someone you travel with.

items.sentence: Guy's a real ___ . He'll chat to anybody.
items.explanation: An outgoing, social person.

items.sentence: When my grandfather arrived in the States, he wanted to become an American ___ as soon as he could.
items.explanation: A legal member of a country.

items.sentence: I do ___ with Kim, but I can't say I really understand what she's going through.
items.explanation: Sympathise means feeling sorry for someone; empathise means sharing their feelings.

items.sentence: I don't think you can really ___ with me when you haven't experienced anything similar.
items.explanation: Empathise requires shared experience or deep understanding.

items.sentence: My parents often ___ children for a few months when I was young.
items.explanation: Foster means looking after a child temporarily.

items.sentence: I was ___ by Frank and Mavis when I was just six weeks old, so they're the only parents I've ever known.
items.explanation: Adopted means legally becoming a child's parent permanently.

items.sentence: To some people, it's very important not to be different from their ___ and to fit in with the group.
items.explanation: Peers are people of the same age or status.

items.sentence: I thought it strange that the invitation didn't say that ___ were invited.
items.explanation: Partners here refers to spouses or significant others.

items.sentence: Jim lost his job because he's got a large number of ___ , including three children and a mother-in-law.
items.explanation: People who rely on you for money.

items.sentence: I never really got on with any of my ___ as I was growing up.
items.explanation: Brothers and sisters.

items.sentence: My ___ in the job left things in a real mess.
items.explanation: The person who held the job before you.

items.sentence: The family home was built by an ___ of mine and six generations of Barretts have lived in it since then.
items.explanation: A relative from a long time ago.

items.sentence: I have decided to leave the company, but I'm sure my ___ will carry on the good work.
items.explanation: The person who takes over your position.

items.sentence: You won't believe me, but I'm a ___ of Isaac Newton, although I don't share the same name.
items.explanation: A person related to someone from the past (child, grandchild, etc.).

exercises.label: F
exercises.instruction: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: I haven't seen much of Cameron since he and Nicola broke ___ .
items.explanation: Break up: end a relationship.

items.sentence: She really loves him and is determined not to let anything come ___ them.
items.explanation: Come between: cause a disagreement/separation.

items.sentence: Why don't we meet ___ here again on the 12th, when I get back from Germany?
items.explanation: Meet up: come together.

items.sentence: I didn't take ___ my stepbrother at first, but after a while we grew closer.
items.explanation: Take to: begin to like.

items.sentence: We should bring everyone ___ and discuss what we're going to do for Grandpa's 80th birthday.
items.explanation: Bring together: gather people.

items.sentence: Being given some responsibility really brought ___ a side of Lindsay's character I hadn't seen before.
items.explanation: Bring out: make a quality show.

items.sentence: I'd like to ask Melanie ___ on a date, but I'm afraid of being rejected.
items.explanation: Ask out: invite on a date.

items.sentence: I had a row with Elizabeth and it just cancelled ___ all the effort I'd put into getting her to like me.
items.explanation: Cancel out: stop something from having an effect.

exercises.label: G
exercises.instruction: Complete using a phrasal verb with a word from box A in the right form and a word from box B.

items.sentence: Guess who I ___ in town. Our old physics teacher, Mr Watson.
items.explanation: Meet someone unexpectedly.

items.sentence: After I got to know Ben a bit better, he started to ___ about his feelings.
items.explanation: Talk more about personal feelings.

items.sentence: The jeans and the top are both fine on their own, but they don't really ___ .
items.explanation: Look good in combination.

items.sentence: Don't ___ the other children in your class just because you're bigger than them.
items.explanation: Treat someone badly/unfairly.

items.sentence: I wish you would just do as you're told and not ___ every time I ask you to do something.
items.explanation: Reply rudely to authority.

items.sentence: You should ___ your parents ___ and see what they think of your plan.
items.explanation: Try to find out someone's opinion.

items.sentence: When the woman fainted in the street, everyone ___ her but no one seemed to know what to do.
items.explanation: Move to a place in a large group.

items.sentence: Michael really ___ his grandfather, doesn't he? They've both got the same red hair.
items.explanation: Look or behave like an older relative.

exercises.label: H
exercises.instruction: Complete the sentences using the words in the box in the correct form.

items.sentence: I'm not aggressive, but if someone attacks me, then I'll ___ back.
items.explanation: Fight back: defend oneself.

items.sentence: The Prime Minister ___ back at his critics and accused them of being biased.
items.explanation: Fire back: reply quickly and angrily.

items.sentence: Don't be surprised if you get into trouble for ___ back to the teacher.
items.explanation: Talk back: answer rudely.

items.sentence: The robber shot at the police, who ___ back.
items.explanation: Fire back: shoot in return.

items.sentence: Nigel rang and he wants you to ___ back as soon as you can.
items.explanation: Call back: return a phone call.

items.sentence: Read the e-mail from Carter Brothers and ___ back to them, explaining where their order is.
items.explanation: Write back: reply to an email/letter.

items.sentence: Lend me enough money for dinner and I'll ___ you back next week.
items.explanation: Pay back: return borrowed money.

items.sentence: I've got a good plan to ___ back at Charlotte for all the trouble she's caused me.
items.explanation: Get back at: take revenge.

exercises.label: I
exercises.instruction: One word in each sentence is incorrect. Underline the incorrect word and write the correct word on the line.

items.sentence: We didn't share the same ___ tongue, but we managed to communicate in English.
items.explanation: Collocation: mother tongue.

items.sentence: When my brother got married, my sister-in-law very quickly became one ___ the family.
items.explanation: Phrase: one of the family.

items.sentence: I sometimes find that ___ speakers of English speak a bit too fast for me.
items.explanation: Collocation: native speakers.

items.sentence: We need to get something for Mum and Dad's ___ anniversary next week.
items.explanation: Collocation: wedding anniversary.

items.sentence: Since tomorrow's a ___ holiday, I thought we might drive up into the mountains.
items.explanation: Collocation: national holiday.

items.sentence: I got an e-mail yesterday from a ___ relative who's researching our family tree.
items.explanation: Collocation: distant relative.

items.sentence: This maths problem should be ___ play to someone with as much talent as you.
items.explanation: Collocation: child's play.

items.sentence: She's not my biological aunt, but she's my aunt ___ marriage.
items.explanation: Phrase: by marriage.

items.sentence: Following the train crash, people concerned about their ___ ones were given a special number to call.
items.explanation: Collocation: loved ones.

items.sentence: The problem of child ___ is much bigger than you might think.
items.explanation: Collocation: child abuse.

items.sentence: It took me a long time to ___ friends with Justine, but it was worth it in the end.
items.explanation: Collocation: make friends.

items.sentence: It's only ___ nature to want to settle down, get married and start a family.
items.explanation: Collocation: human nature.

exercises.label: J
exercises.instruction: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: The usual western idea of marriage is that you meet someone, ___ in love...
items.explanation: Fall in love.

items.sentence: For those born ___ some cultures, however...
items.explanation: Born into a culture.

items.sentence: ...it's possible ___ the young person getting married...
items.explanation: Possible for someone.

items.sentence: ...not to meet their future bride or groom ___ person until the day of the wedding.
items.explanation: In person.

items.sentence: Indeed, a young person might be promised to another ___ a child...
items.explanation: As a child.

items.sentence: ...it's not unusual for a generation ___ to open up...
items.explanation: Generation gap.

items.sentence: However, if the partners treat each other ___ respect...
items.explanation: Treat with respect.

items.sentence: ...and recognise that they are both ___ equal value...
items.explanation: Of equal value.

exercises.label: K
exercises.instruction: For each question, write one word which can be used in all three sentences.

items.sentence: Oh, tell Carrie that I send my ___ when you see her. / When my mum and dad met, my mum says it was ___ at first sight. / My ___ for travelling comes from the fact that we moved around a lot.
items.explanation: send love, love at first sight, love for something.

items.sentence: One of the reasons why Damian and I get on so well is that we have a lot in ___ . / It was ___ practice to start building before getting all the permits. / There are some words which are ___ to a number of the languages spoken in the area.
items.explanation: have in common, common practice, common to.

items.sentence: I don't really see myself settling down any time in the ___ future. / People came from ___ and far to attend the celebrity wedding of the year. / We just about managed to get to the airport on time, but it was a ___ thing.
items.explanation: near future, near and far, a near thing.

items.sentence: The two countries are similar with ___ to the divorce rates. / ...I don't think we're any different from other couples in that ___ . / Todd lost a lot of people's ___ when they saw how he treats his wife.
items.explanation: with respect to, in that respect, lose respect.

items.sentence: One of the important ___ of the law is that it recognises the rights of unmarried partners. / ...asked if he had any distinguishing ___ . / I was quite impressed by the safety ___ on Bill's new car.
items.explanation: important features, distinguishing features, safety features.

items.sentence: We ___ our son while he was at university... / My father ___ Chelsea, and so did his father before him... / ...surprised by the number of people who ___ the death penalty.
items.explanation: supported financially, supported a team, supported an idea.

exercises.label: L
exercises.instruction: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: Vivian and I usually agree, but we're not always on the ___ wavelength.
items.explanation: On the same wavelength.

items.sentence: I don't know Pauline that well, but I'm on good ___ with her sister.
items.explanation: On good terms.

items.sentence: I know I made a mistake, but I'm only ___ , after all.
items.explanation: Only human.

items.sentence: My brother and Jack got on like a ___ on fire when they first met.
items.explanation: Get on like a house on fire.

items.sentence: It's a shame to fall out so badly with your own ___ and blood.
items.explanation: Flesh and blood.

items.sentence: Life's very easy for you. You were born with a ___ spoon in your mouth.
items.explanation: Silver spoon in your mouth.

items.sentence: Jill and Emily are so alike. They're like two ___ in a pod.
items.explanation: Two peas in a pod.

items.sentence: I don't think we're ever going to ___ eye to eye on this issue.
items.explanation: See eye to eye.

items.sentence: When something like that happens, it certainly ___ your own problems in perspective.
items.explanation: Put in perspective.

items.sentence: I'm avoiding Mr Hunter because I think I'm in his bad ___ .
items.explanation: In someone's bad books.

exercises.label: M
exercises.instruction: Complete the sentences by changing the form of the word in capitals.

items.sentence: Theo and his brother are always together. They're completely ___ .
items.explanation: Inseparable: cannot be separated.

items.sentence: It's very ___ of you to notice that Sean's not his usual self.
items.explanation: Perceptive: quick to notice things.

items.sentence: There was a long legal battle over the ___ , but in the end I did get quite a bit of money.
items.explanation: Inheritance: money/property received from someone who died.

items.sentence: I think saying that all black people are lazy is a very ___ remark, to be honest.
items.explanation: Racist: relating to racism.

items.sentence: Despite his ___ lack of concern, I think Gabriel really does care what other people think of him.
items.explanation: Apparent: seeming to be true.

items.sentence: Don't feel that you're being ___ to Sharon if you tell me what's bothering you about her.
items.explanation: Disloyal: not loyal.

items.sentence: James got into trouble for ___ a police officer.
items.explanation: Impersonate: pretend to be someone else.

items.sentence: If you have any complaints about a neighbour, take them to your residents' ___ ...
items.explanation: Association: an organized group of people.

items.sentence: Marvin used to be quite wild, but he's ___ calm these days.
items.explanation: Relatively: to a certain degree, compared to other times.

items.sentence: According to Lionel, losing his job and then getting divorced were ___ , but I think there's probably a link.
items.explanation: Unconnected: not joined or related.

items.sentence: Young animals very quickly form a strong ___ to their mothers.
items.explanation: Attachment: a feeling of love and need for another.

items.sentence: What I don't like about school uniform is that it completely destroys all ___ .
items.explanation: Individuality: the qualities that make a person different from others.

exercises.label: N
exercises.instruction: Use the word given in capitals to form a word that fits in the space.

items.sentence: Very little in our lives prepares us for ___ .
items.explanation: Parenthood: the state of being a parent.

items.sentence: ...and all sorts of ___ demands are placed on you.
items.explanation: Unfamiliar: not known or recognized.

items.sentence: How we ourselves were treated by our parents in our ___ ...
items.explanation: Youth: the time of life when you are young.

items.sentence: ...can have an ___ effect on who we become as parents.
items.explanation: Appreciable: large enough to be noticed.

items.sentence: Our own ___ of how our parents responded to us...
items.explanation: Observation: the act of watching carefully.

items.sentence: ...creates a model of parenting that is ___ connected to the kind of parents we become.
items.explanation: Intimately: in a very close way.

items.sentence: It's not uncommon for people to show the same child-rearing ___ as their own parents.
items.explanation: Characteristics: typical features or qualities.

items.sentence: If your father was an ___ figure who always seemed too busy...
items.explanation: Unsympathetic: not showing kindness or understanding.

items.sentence: If your mother was utterly ___ in her devotion...
items.explanation: Selfless: caring more about other people than yourself.

items.sentence: ...and do all that is ___ possible for your offspring.
items.explanation: Humanly: by human means.

exercises.label: O
exercises.instruction: The prefix re-, as in reappear, can go before verbs, nouns and adjectives to mean 'again'. Tick the words below which can form words using re-.

items.sentence: arrange
build
consider
count
create
cycle
develop
dial
do
fill
fresh
heat
join
new
pass
print
produce
say
think
train
trial
use
worry
write

exercises.label: P
exercises.instruction: Complete the sentences using words formed in exercise O in the correct form.

items.sentence: I'm beginning to ___ my plans for the future.
items.explanation: Consider again.

items.sentence: Why don't you ___ the soup in the microwave?
items.explanation: Heat again.

items.sentence: Don't forget to ___ your passport, or you won't be able to come on holiday!
items.explanation: Make new again (passport/license).

items.sentence: You've spilt coffee on my notebook! Now I'm going to have to ___ my whole essay!
items.explanation: Write again.

items.sentence: When my mum lost her job, she decided to ___ as an engineer.
items.explanation: Train again for a new job.

items.sentence: Empty that bit of Coke down the sink and then you can ___ the bottle with water.
items.explanation: Fill again.

items.sentence: The election result was so close that they've decided to ___ the votes.
items.explanation: Count again.

items.sentence: I left my friends in Paris while I explored the area and then I ___ them a few days later.
items.explanation: Join again.

items.sentence: Scientists are hoping to ___ the conditions that existed on Earth millions of years ago.
items.explanation: Create again (simulate).

items.sentence: You'll easily pass the exam if you can ___ the very good performance you put in during the mock exams.
items.explanation: Produce again.
